svg.text: AWS Config · EventBridge · Step Functions · Lambda · Bedrock · DynamoDB · GitHub Actions · Terraform
Normalizes and enriches the Config event
Extracts resource type, ID, rule name
Triggered on every PR and push to main
1. AWS Config evaluates resources against 3 managed rules continuously.
A resource modification triggers a NON_COMPLIANT evaluation.
2. EventBridge captures the compliance change event and starts
the Step Functions state machine automatically.
3. Detect Drift normalizes the raw Config payload into a structured
drift record with resource type, ID, and rule name.
4. Query History reads prior incidents of the same drift type
from DynamoDB and writes an initial incident record.
5. Call Bedrock invokes Claude Sonnet 4 on Amazon Bedrock,
returning severity, intent assessment, and remediation reasoning.
6. Generate Terraform produces a drift type-specific HCL plan.
7. Validate & Escalate updates the DynamoDB incident, publishes an
SNS alert, and opens a GitHub PR if a token is configured.
• Security group open SSH — CRITICAL — auto-triggered via Config
• S3 bucket public read — HIGH — auto-triggered via Config
• IAM admin policy — CRITICAL — auto-triggered via Config
• Unencrypted EBS volume — MEDIUM — CLI trigger (test event)
Full execution time: ~12–14 seconds per pipeline run
5 Step Functions executions · 49 Lambda invocations · 15 Bedrock calls
Bedrock over direct API: IAM auth eliminates API keys; entire architecture
stays inside AWS; native CloudWatch metrics on LLM invocations.
DynamoDB over PostgreSQL: different drift types carry different attributes.
DynamoDB handles semi-structured data natively without schema migrations.
Step Functions over single Lambda: per-step error handling, retry logic,
and a visual workflow diagram as both debugging tool and demo artifact.
One IAM role per Lambda: each function is scoped to only the services it
touches — least privilege enforced at the architecture level.
Contract-first design: Step Functions ASL defined first; DynamoDB schema
and Lambda handlers derived from it in pipeline order.
Bedrock — Claude Sonnet 4 · 15 calls · ~22k input + 12k output tokens ........... ~$0.20–0.50
Total: under $1 (infrastructure provisioned for 1–2 days, then destroyed)
TerraDriftGuard — Designed and implemented by Malik Hamdane — 2026
